concerns.analysis: The main content of page 24, which consists of a full-page map titled 'Figure 5. Wastewater discharges', is completely missing from the output. Instead, the pipeline incorrectly attached the figure caption to the small Moffatt & Nichol logo on the page, completely omitting the actual map.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: A large map displaying 'Figure 5. Wastewater discharges' covering the majority of page 24.
concerns.output_quote: The map image is absent. The output contains an image block describing 'Logo for Moffatt & Nichol' with the caption 'Figure 5. Wastewater discharges'.

concerns.analysis: Table 5 spans multiple pages (17-19) in the source PDF with repeated headers. However, it was extracted as three separate table objects in the JSON (one on each page) rather than being merged into a single continuous table, which violates the multi-page table extraction rule.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Table 5. Summary of Wastewater Discharges spanning pages 17, 18, and 19 with repeated headers
concerns.output_quote: Three separate table blocks are created on pages 17, 18, and 19, each starting with the header row 'Permit', 'Owner', 'Facility', etc.

concerns.analysis: The Table of Contents and List of Tables on page 2 were extracted as lists that retain the dotted leaders and page numbers from the source PDF. This is explicitly called out as a major defect because page references are meaningless in continuous HTML output without functional anchor links.
concerns.location: Page 2, Table of Contents and List of Tables

concerns.analysis: The text 'MOFFATT & NICHOL' from the footer logo appears twice consecutively in the JSON output on several pages, whereas it is only present once in the visual footer of the source PDF.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Footer text on pages 21, 22, and 24: 'MOFFATT & NICHOL'

concerns.description: Table split across page break(s): 3 table(s) with matching structure across consecutive pages
concerns.analysis: split_table_count=3. Examples: pages 14-15: 5 cols (fid_1, co_name...); pages 17-18: 14 cols (permit, owner...); pages 18-19: 14 cols (permit, owner...). Tables spanning multiple pages were extracted as separate table elements. In HTML, these should be one continuous table to maintain accessibility and reading order.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (52): NEUSE BASIN MODELING - DATA COLLECTION DRAFT REPORT; April 2009; - ii -
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=52, pages_affected=24/24. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: 5 bare URL(s) in text not converted to hyperlinks: http://www.agr.state.nc.us/stats/release/WU2007; http://www.ncwater.org/whichsystem.php; <http://lwf.ncdc.noaa.gov/oa/ncdc.html>; <http://www.agcensus.usda.gov/Publications/2007/index.asp>; http://www.census.gov/prod/www/abs/decennial/index.htm>
concerns.analysis: bare_url_count=5. URLs appear as plain text instead of clickable links. Per NCDIT criteria, link text not wrapped in href is a major defect.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 1 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th=0, datarow_as_header=0, no_th_tables=1. Tables use <th> for data values, have a data row mismarked as a header row, or lack <th> elements entirely. Screen readers rely on <th> to announce column/row context.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 52 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (52 truncated mid-sentence) — e.g. p1: "...iangles that display white silhouettes of a flying"; p1: "... stylized, geometric blue and gray letters 'M' and"; p2: "...tions with white silhouettes of a bird over water,"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=52 (truncated=52, placeholder=0, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.

concerns.description: Degraded image quality: 2 image(s) used fallback rendering instead of direct extraction
concerns.analysis: fallback_rendered_images=2. Examples: p20: "A map titled "Neuse Basin Modeling - Data Collecti"; p23: "Map from the 'Neuse Basin Modeling - Data Collecti". These images were not directly extracted from the PDF — instead, an approximate page region was rendered as a PNG. Fallback images may be cropped incorrectly, show the wrong content, or miss meaningful visual elements.

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 2 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=2, examples=['http://www.agr.state.nc.us/plantindustry/NurseryBook/search.', 'http://nc.water.usgs.gov/infodata/wateruse/data/Data_Tables_']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: Source PDF contains 3 instance(s) of blue-styled text without hyperlink annotations. These appear to be intended as hyperlinks but were not annotated in the PDF (likely lost during Word-to-PDF export). This is a source document limitation, not a pipeline defect. Examples: page 11: "http://www.agr.state.nc.us/plantindustry/"; page 11: "NurseryBook/search.asp"; page 11: "http://nc.water.usgs.gov/infodata/wateruse/data/Da"
concerns.analysis: orphaned_blue_text_count=3. Blue text without link annotations detected via PyMuPDF color extraction.
